context7.instructions: External library documentation lookups. Use when doc-search doesn't have the library indexed.

chrome-devtools-visible.instructions: Visible Chrome on Windows host. Wrapper resolves host.docker.internal to IP (Chrome 66+ rejects non-IP Host headers). Start Chrome: 'solemd chrome start' or Start-ChromeDebug.ps1.

supabase-solemd.instructions: SQL-based access to ALL project schemas (solemd, vocab, make, web, wiki, zotero). Query live schema first - it's authoritative.

supabase-umls.instructions: Query UMLS terminology data. Contains MRCONSO, MRREL, and semantic type tables.

code-search.instructions: Primary tool for code exploration and navigation. Use the streamlined 11-tool surface: search_code, inspect_symbol, trace_flow, file_context, dependents, analyze_impact, find_bridge_functions, graph_overview, slice_build, slice_view, index_status. Default exploration flow: search_code(mode=auto, detail=skeleton, prefer_source=true) -> take returned symbol_ref and symbol_uid -> inspect_symbol(symbol_ref=...) -> trace_flow(from_ref=..., to_ref=...) or trace_flow(from_uid=..., to_uid=...) when a path question appears. Use detail=card to screen many candidates and detail=full only after the target symbol/file is selected. Promote good hits into persistent task context with slice_build(task=...) or slice_build(promote_refs=[symbol_ref, symbol_uid, ...]), then use slice_view(slice_id, detail=...) to escalate context. Read matched_on, rank_explain, source_bias_filtered, neighborhood, and unresolved call-site telemetry before broadening search. Use index_status reindex_mode=all|content|graph to target rebuild scope.

doc-search.instructions: Query external library documentation using hybrid search (BM25 + semantic). 30+ libraries indexed: React, Next.js, Supabase, Langfuse, FastAPI, Pydantic, etc. Use resolve-library-id to find library, then query-docs to search. For exact API names, use exactMatch=true. See /doc-search skill for full guide.

neo4j.instructions: Neo4j graph database — direct Cypher queries via official Neo4j MCP server. Use for schema inspection (get-schema), read queries (read-cypher), and write operations (write-cypher). Code-search handles LLM-facing structural workflows via inspect_symbol, trace_flow, and analyze_impact. Use this server for direct ad-hoc Cypher.
